# Pricing Agent — Follow-up Edits & Sheets Export — Design Spec

**Status:** READY TO IMPLEMENT — both reviews CLEAR (2026-04-30)
**ClickUp:** [86b9pgepx — Pricing agent not working as intended](https://app.clickup.com/t/86b9pgepx) (P1, AI-den/P1 Soon)
**Generated by:** /office-hours (premise + 3 alternatives + adversarial reviewer) → /plan-eng-review (4 architecture decisions + test gap fill)

## Problem

The pricing agent runs the initial calculation correctly (uploaded Excel or inline items) but cannot revise its own output on follow-up turns. When a user says "increase GBP by 5% and regenerate" or "change SKU ABC-123 to $149," the agent has no structured prior-turn state to operate on, so it either re-asks for the upload, hallucinates revised numbers, or silently produces nothing.

**Root cause (verified in code):**

1. `backend/app/mcp/tools/pricing_tools.py` — `pricing_convert_execute` and `pricing_export_execute` compute `results: list[ConversionResult]` in memory, write Excel + NetSuite CSV files, return a 10-row `preview` (list of dicts) plus file IDs. The full input list and config snapshot are not persisted in any structured, reload-able form.
2. `backend/app/services/chat/orchestrator.py:2273` — `_on_tool_intercepted` callback writes `event_data.get("columns", [])` / `event_data.get("rows", [])` into `result_cache`. Pricing's `task_output` event has neither key, so the cache stores `columns=[], rows=[], row_count=0`. The follow-up turn sees an empty cache.
3. `backend/app/services/chat/result_cache.py:CachedResult` — schema is hardcoded to flat tabular data (`columns: list[str]`, `rows: list[list[Any]]`). No slot for typed payloads.
4. There is no Sheets export path from pricing today. `sheets_create_execute` and `write_range` exist but pricing doesn't compose them.

Secondary observation worth flagging (not blocking): `pricing_tools.py` builds a 10-row markdown table into `response_instruction` and tells the LLM to print it verbatim. This contradicts `feedback_no_llm_numbers.md` and Mistakes #41 in `CLAUDE.md`. The fix below avoids re-introducing this pattern on the revise path.

## Scope

**In scope (v1):**
- Cache pricing inputs + applied overrides + config snapshot per session, typed. **Row data lives in the saved Excel file (durable via TaskFileService), not in Redis** — cache stores `excel_file_id`, headers, and row count. `pricing_to_sheets` re-parses the Excel file on demand to write to the spreadsheet. Keeps Redis hot-data footprint flat.
- Per-result-type cache slots so a SuiteQL/BigQuery turn between two pricing turns doesn't evict the pricing state.
- New `pricing_revise` MCP tool that reads the cached state, applies LLM-passed overrides, re-runs the engine server-side, writes new Excel + NetSuite CSV outputs.
- New `pricing_to_sheets` MCP tool that reads the cached pricing state, parses the saved Excel file, and writes to Google Sheets server-side (LLM never sees the row data).
- Engine extension: per-currency multiplicative uplift applied PRE-rounding so charm-price rules (`nearest_9`, `nearest_990`, `nearest_50`) still produce charm prices after a "+5% on GBP" override.
- **Anti-pattern cleanup (folded in per eng review D2):** remove the `response_instruction` markdown-table builder from `pricing_convert_execute` and `pricing_export_execute`. Both tools currently tell the LLM to print a 10-row preview verbatim, violating CLAUDE.md Mistakes #41 / `feedback_no_llm_numbers.md`. The frontend `task-output-card.tsx` already renders the preview; the LLM should not re-emit numbers.
- Knowledge profile updates (`pricing.yaml`, `google_sheets.yaml`) so the agent knows when to call `pricing_revise` vs. `pricing_export` vs. `pricing_to_sheets`, and that pricing previews render automatically.

**Out of scope (flag as follow-ups):**
- Scenario-compare ("show with 5% uplift, now with 10%, now go back to 5%"). Cache TTL is 30 min and max 6 results post-bump — works briefly, degrades. Defer until requested.
- Per-row override syntax richer than what the engine already supports (e.g., conditional uplifts on product groups). v1 supports the override surface listed below.
- Spreadsheet update-in-place ("update the same sheet I made earlier"). v1 always creates a new sheet.

## Approach

**Reframe:** the ticket proposed caching pricing OUTPUT rows so follow-ups could transform the table. We're caching INPUTS instead: the SKU list, applied overrides, and config snapshot. The pricing engine is deterministic and in-memory, so re-running it on every revise is cheap. The LLM passes intent (`{percent_uplift: 0.05, target_currencies: ["GBP"]}`) — never raw prices. This keeps the engine as the single source of truth and respects the existing "no LLM numbers" rule.

**Cumulative semantics (locked):**

- `sku_price_changes`, `skus_to_add`, `skus_to_remove`, `currencies_to_add`, `currencies_to_remove` — **accumulate** across revises by mutating an `effective_items` / `effective_currencies` set in the payload.
- `percent_uplift`, `fx_rate_overrides`, `vat_rate_overrides`, `rounding_overrides` — **replace per key**. Latest revise's `{GBP: 0.05}` wins on GBP, but a previous `{EUR: 0.03}` persists in the effective config.
- `applied_overrides` audit list grows append-only for traceability; the engine sees the merged effective state, not a stack.
- "Reset to original" is a `pricing_revise` call with `overrides.reset = true` — clears `effective_items` back to the seed and drops all uplift/FX/VAT/rounding overrides. Seeded items + base config are preserved separately in the payload for this purpose.

**Per-SKU conflict resolution (locked, eng review D3 — latest-wins):**

- Two `sku_price_changes` for the same SKU across revises → latest USD price wins. The earlier price is retained in the audit log only.
- `skus_to_add` for an SKU already in `effective_items` → silent no-op (logged as `{ignored: "sku already present"}`). Not an error — the LLM may surface a duplicate add when the user says "also include SKU-X" without realizing it's there.
- `skus_to_remove` for an SKU not in `effective_items` → silent no-op (logged similarly).
- `skus_to_remove` for an SKU added in a prior revise → SKU is dropped (remove wins, regardless of add order).
- Within a single revise call, the order of operations is: `skus_to_remove` first, then `skus_to_add`, then `sku_price_changes`. Same-call add+remove for the same SKU = SKU is added with the new price.

**Empty effective_items contract:** if `skus_to_remove` empties `effective_items`, `pricing_revise` returns `{"error": true, "message": "No SKUs left after removals. Use reset=true to start over, or add SKUs back."}` rather than calling the engine with an empty list.

**Four layers of change:**

1. **Cache schema** — add `payload: dict | None = None` to `CachedResult`, add `get_latest_result_by_type(conversation_id, result_type)` helper, change eviction to keep the most recent entry per `result_type` before evicting cross-type. Bump `MAX_RESULTS_PER_CONVERSATION` from 3 to 6 to accommodate mixed-tool sessions.
2. **Engine extension** — `PricingEngine.convert_batch(items, config, *, uplift_by_currency: dict[str, Decimal] | None = None)`. The uplift multiplier is applied to the post-VAT, pre-rounding value inside `convert_single` so charm-price rounding rules still terminate on the charm digits. Default `None` → existing behavior.
3. **New tools:**
   - `pricing_revise(overrides)` — reads latest pricing payload, applies overrides, calls engine with `uplift_by_currency`, writes new outputs, emits `task_output` SSE event identical in shape to the initial run. **Does NOT include `response_instruction` markdown table** (avoids re-introducing the LLM-numbers anti-pattern; see secondary observation below).
   - `pricing_to_sheets()` — reads latest pricing payload, calls `create_spreadsheet` + `write_range` server-side using the FULL row set from cache (not the 10-row preview), emits a `sheets_link` SSE event with the URL. LLM never sees the rows.
4. **Knowledge profile** — `pricing.yaml` teaches the agent the override mapping for natural-language follow-ups and the `pricing_to_sheets` chain trigger.

## Backend

### Schema change: `backend/app/services/chat/result_cache.py`

Add an optional `payload` field to `CachedResult`:

```python
@dataclass
class CachedResult:
    message_id: str
    conversation_id: str
    result_type: str   # "suiteql" | "financial_report" | "bigquery" | "saved_search" | "pricing"
    columns: list[str]
    rows: list[list[Any]]
    row_count: int
    summary: dict[str, Any] | None = None
    query_text: str = ""
    payload: dict[str, Any] | None = None   # NEW: typed per-result_type state
    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
```

`to_json` / `from_json` round-trip the field. Defaults to `None` so existing cache entries deserialize unchanged.

**New helper — `get_latest_result_by_type(conversation_id, result_type) -> CachedResult | None`:**
Filters cached entries by `result_type` before picking the most-recent. `pricing_revise` and `pricing_to_sheets` call this with `result_type="pricing"` so a SuiteQL/BigQuery turn between two pricing turns doesn't trick the executor into reading the wrong cache entry. `get_latest_result` (untyped) stays for callers that genuinely want any-most-recent.

**Eviction policy change:**
Current eviction (`cache_result`, lines 78-90) deletes the oldest entry whenever count exceeds `MAX_RESULTS_PER_CONVERSATION`. Change to:
1. Bump `MAX_RESULTS_PER_CONVERSATION` from 3 to 6.
2. Before evicting, group entries by `result_type` and pin the most recent of each type. Evict from the unpinned set, oldest first.
3. This guarantees the latest pricing entry survives across mixed-tool sessions, even if the pricing run was followed by a SuiteQL + BigQuery + financial-report sequence.

**Migration thought:** in-flight cached entries written before the deploy lack `payload`. `from_json(**d)` will raise if `payload` is required, so we keep it optional with a default. Verified by adding a regression test that loads an old-shape JSON blob.

**Cache-key timing:** the orchestrator currently writes cache entries under `message_id="pending"` (orchestrator.py:2283-ish), then the assistant message persists under a real ID. For pricing this is fine because `pricing_revise` and `pricing_to_sheets` use `get_latest_result_by_type`, not specific IDs. We do NOT expose `message_id` as a tool parameter in v1 — it's an implementation detail.

### New tool: `backend/app/mcp/tools/pricing_revise.py` (paired with executor in `pricing_tools.py`)

```python
TOOL_DEFINITION = {
    "name": "pricing_revise",
    "description": (
        "Revise the most recent pricing result with the requested overrides and "
        "regenerate Excel + NetSuite CSV outputs. Use for follow-up edits like "
        "'increase GBP by 5%', 'change SKU ABC-123 USD to 149', 'use nearest_50 "
        "rounding for JPY only', 'add EUR and CAD', 'remove SKU X'. "
        "DO NOT call this for the first pricing run — use pricing_convert "
        "(uploaded Excel) or pricing_export (inline items) instead. "
        "All numeric fields are interpreted with full Decimal precision; pass "
        "values as JSON numbers and the executor will convert via Decimal(str(v))."
    ),
    "input_schema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "reset": {
                "type": "boolean",
                "description": "If true, drop all accumulated overrides and revert to the original seeded items + base config. Other override fields are ignored when reset=true.",
            },
            "overrides": {
                "type": "object",
                "description": "Changes to apply. All fields optional; combine freely.",
                "properties": {
                    "sku_price_changes": {
                        "type": "array",
                        "items": {"type": "object", "properties": {
                            "sku": {"type": "string"},
                            "usd_price": {"type": "number"},
                        }, "required": ["sku", "usd_price"]},
                        "description": "Replace USD base prices for specific SKUs.",
                    },
                    "skus_to_remove": {
                        "type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"},
                    },
                    "skus_to_add": {
                        "type": "array",
                        "items": {"type": "object", "properties": {
                            "sku": {"type": "string"},
                            "usd_price": {"type": "number"},
                            "item_name": {"type": "string"},
                        }, "required": ["sku", "usd_price"]},
                    },
                    "percent_uplift": {
                        "type": "object",
                        "description": "Multiplicative adjustment by currency. e.g. {GBP: 0.05} = +5% on GBP only.",
                        "additionalProperties": {"type": "number"},
                    },
                    "fx_rate_overrides": {
                        "type": "object",
                        "additionalProperties": {"type": "number"},
                    },
                    "vat_rate_overrides": {
                        "type": "object",
                        "additionalProperties": {"type": "number"},
                    },
                    "rounding_overrides": {
                        "type": "object",
                        "description": "Per-currency rounding rule swap (e.g. {JPY: 'nearest_50'}).",
                        "additionalProperties": {"type": "string"},
                    },
                    "currencies_to_add": {
                        "type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"},
                        "description": "Add currencies to the output set. Tenant config must already define their FX/VAT/rounding.",
                    },
                    "currencies_to_remove": {
                        "type": "array", "items": {"type": "string"},
                    },
                },
            },
        },
        "required": ["overrides"],
    },
}
```

**Executor responsibilities:**

1. Read latest pricing payload via `get_latest_result_by_type(conversation_id, "pricing")`. If miss/expired, return:
   ```json
   {"error": true, "message": "No prior pricing state in this conversation (cache expired or no pricing run yet). Re-run pricing_convert with the upload, or pricing_export with inline items."}
   ```
2. Deserialize `payload` into a Pydantic model:
   ```python
   class PricingState(BaseModel):
       seed_items: list[PricingInput]            # original input, never mutated
       effective_items: list[PricingInput]       # after accumulated SKU mutations
       effective_currencies: list[str]           # after currencies_to_add / _to_remove
       effective_fx_overrides: dict[str, Decimal]
       effective_vat_overrides: dict[str, Decimal]
       effective_rounding_overrides: dict[str, str]
       effective_uplift_by_currency: dict[str, Decimal]
       applied_overrides_log: list[dict[str, Any]]  # append-only audit
       excel_file_id: str                         # latest saved Excel (for pricing_to_sheets to re-parse)
       netsuite_csv_file_id: str                  # latest saved CSV (for download)
       header_columns: list[str]                  # column order: SKU, Item Name, USD, then per-currency
       row_count: int                             # last engine run row count
   ```
   **Row data is NOT in the payload** — `pricing_to_sheets` re-parses the Excel file via `excel_file_id`. Keeps Redis lean per eng review D1.
   **Base tenant config is NOT in the payload** — fetched fresh on every revise via `get_config(db, tenant_id)` per eng review D4. Override deltas are the only config state that lives in cache. Audit lineage of the original config used at seed-time lives in `PricingConversionLog.config_snapshot` (DB) where it belongs.
3. **Fetch fresh tenant config:** `current_config = await get_config(db, tenant_id)` (per eng review D4). This is the base for the engine call; effective overrides apply on top.
4. If `reset=true`: rebuild `effective_items` from `seed_items`, drop all override dicts (uplift/FX/VAT/rounding). Effective config = `current_config` with no overrides. Skip step 5.
5. Apply incoming `overrides` to the effective state in this order: `skus_to_remove` → `skus_to_add` → `sku_price_changes` → currency ops → uplift/FX/VAT/rounding (per locked semantics above):
   - **Decimal precision:** every numeric value (`usd_price`, `percent_uplift` values, `fx_rate_overrides` values, `vat_rate_overrides` values) is converted via `Decimal(str(v))` to avoid float round-off.
   - `skus_to_remove`: drop matching SKUs from `effective_items`. Missing SKUs → silent no-op, logged.
   - `skus_to_add`: append items not already in `effective_items`. Duplicates → silent no-op, logged.
   - `sku_price_changes`: replace `usd_price` for matching SKUs in `effective_items`. Missing SKUs → silent no-op, logged. Latest-wins across revises (already in `effective_items`).
   - **Empty-list guard:** after these three ops, if `effective_items` is empty, return `{"error": true, "message": "No SKUs left after removals. Use reset=true to start over, or add SKUs back."}` and skip the rest.
   - `currencies_to_add` validates each requested currency exists in `seed_config.currencies` (tenant config). If not, return:
     ```json
     {"error": true, "message": "Currency XYZ is not configured for this tenant. Add it in Settings → Pricing first, then retry."}
     ```
   - `currencies_to_remove` filters from `effective_currencies` (no-op if not present).
   - `percent_uplift` / `fx_rate_overrides` / `vat_rate_overrides` / `rounding_overrides` REPLACE per key (latest revise wins on that key).
   - Append the override dict to `applied_overrides_log` with timestamp.
6. Compose effective config: start from `current_config` (fetched fresh in step 3), apply effective FX/VAT/rounding overrides, filter `currencies` to `effective_currencies`.
7. Call `PricingEngine.convert_batch(effective_items, effective_config, uplift_by_currency=effective_uplift_by_currency)`. Engine applies uplift PRE-rounding (see Engine extension below) so charm-price rules still terminate correctly.
8. Write new Excel + NetSuite CSV via `TemplateFiller.generate_default_output` + `generate_netsuite_csv`. Same file-save path as `pricing_export`.
9. Emit `task_output` SSE event with `preview` (10 rows for UI) + `output_files`. **Do NOT include a `response_instruction` markdown table** — the existing card renders the preview without LLM re-emission.
10. Set `payload` on the new cached entry to the updated effective state — including the new `excel_file_id`, `netsuite_csv_file_id`, `header_columns`, and `row_count`. Set under `event_data["pricing_state"]` for the orchestrator interceptor (see below). Row data stays in the Excel file, not in cache. Base tenant config is NOT cached — fetched fresh on every revise.
11. Audit-log to `PricingConversionLog` with `applied_overrides_log` appended in `config_snapshot`. The snapshot persists the FRESH config used for this revise, so the audit trail captures both the original seed run AND every revise's config-at-time-of-run.

**Conversation_id plumbing:** `pricing_revise` (and `pricing_to_sheets` and `pricing_convert` / `pricing_export` for the seed-payload write) need `conversation_id` to call `get_latest_result_by_type` and to write the new cache entry. The orchestrator already has `session.id` (orchestrator.py:2284) and passes it into the cache write callback. For the executor side, add `conversation_id` to the `context` dict the orchestrator passes to tool executors, alongside the existing `db` / `tenant_id` / `actor_id`. Single-line change in the executor dispatch path. `result_reference_tool.py` shows the pattern (takes `conversation_id` directly).

### Engine extension: per-currency pre-rounding uplift

`backend/app/services/pricing_engine.py` — extend the convert path:

```python
def convert_batch(
    items: list[PricingInput],
    config: TenantPricingConfig,
    *,
    uplift_by_currency: dict[str, Decimal] | None = None,
) -> list[PricingOutput]:
    ...

def convert_single(
    item: PricingInput,
    config: TenantPricingConfig,
    *,
    uplift_by_currency: dict[str, Decimal] | None = None,
) -> PricingOutput:
    # ... existing FX + VAT pipeline ...
    pre_round_value = post_vat_value
    if uplift_by_currency and currency_code in uplift_by_currency:
        pre_round_value = pre_round_value * (Decimal("1") + uplift_by_currency[currency_code])
    final_price = apply_rounding(pre_round_value, currency_config.rounding_rule)
    # ... build CurrencyResult ...
```

Default `None` keeps existing callers unchanged. With `{GBP: Decimal('0.05')}` set, GBP prices are multiplied by 1.05 BEFORE the rounding rule runs — so a `nearest_990` GBP item still ends in 490/990, not 514.05.

### New tool: `backend/app/mcp/tools/pricing_to_sheets.py`

```python
TOOL_DEFINITION = {
    "name": "pricing_to_sheets",
    "description": (
        "Export the most recent pricing result to a new Google Sheet. Use when "
        "the user asks to export, share, or send pricing to a Sheet. Reads the "
        "cached pricing state server-side — does NOT need row data passed in. "
        "Returns the spreadsheet URL. The Excel + NetSuite CSV downloads from the "
        "prior pricing run are preserved separately."
    ),
    "input_schema": {
        "type": "object",
        "properties": {
            "title": {
                "type": "string",
                "description": "Optional spreadsheet title. Defaults to 'Pricing Export — <YYYY-MM-DD>'.",
            },
        },
    },
}
```

**Executor responsibilities:**
1. **Read the Google Sheets connector first** (`provider="google_sheets"`, status="active"). If missing, return `{"error": true, "message": "Google Sheets connector not configured. Set it up in Settings → Connectors first."}`. Connector check before cache check so users get the right setup-vs-session error.
2. Read latest pricing payload via `get_latest_result_by_type(conversation_id, "pricing")`. Cache-miss error: `{"error": true, "message": "No pricing run in this conversation yet. Run pricing_convert (with an upload) or pricing_export (with inline items) first, then export to Sheets."}`
3. Re-parse the saved Excel file via `TaskFileService.get_file(db, tenant_id, payload.excel_file_id)` + `openpyxl.load_workbook(io.BytesIO(content))`. Read the active sheet's row 1 as headers, rows 2..max_row as data.
4. Call `create_spreadsheet(credentials, title or default, shared_drive_id)` then `write_range(credentials, spreadsheet_id, "A1", [headers, *data_rows])`.
5. If user has an email and the sheet is not in a Shared Drive, call `share_spreadsheet`. Same logic as `sheets_create_execute`.
6. Emit a `sheets_link` SSE event (existing event type, rendered by `sheets-link-card.tsx`) with `{spreadsheet_id, url}`.
7. Return `{success: true, url, sku_count: payload.row_count}` to the LLM. The agent mentions the sheet is ready; the URL renders via the link card.

**Latency note:** Excel parse adds ~0.5-1s on a 5,000-SKU sheet. Acceptable for a one-off export. If users complain, we can add a per-tenant cap or async export job — out of scope for v1.

### Orchestrator wiring: `backend/app/services/chat/orchestrator.py`

Update `_on_tool_intercepted` (around line 2273) to populate `payload` and `result_type` when the tool is pricing-related. `pricing_to_sheets` does NOT update the cache — it's a read-only consumer.

```python
# Pricing tools that WRITE the pricing cache entry. Add new pricing-write tools here.
# Read-only consumers (pricing_to_sheets) do NOT belong in this set — they read via
# get_latest_result_by_type and never mutate the cache.
_PRICING_WRITE_TOOLS = {"pricing_convert", "pricing_export", "pricing_revise"}

def _on_tool_intercepted(tool_name: str, event_type_str: str, event_data: dict):
    columns = event_data.get("columns", [])
    rows = event_data.get("rows", [])
    payload = None
    result_type = _result_type_for(tool_name)
    if tool_name in _PRICING_WRITE_TOOLS:
        payload = event_data.get("pricing_state")
        result_type = "pricing"
    pc = CachedResult(
        ...,
        result_type=result_type,
        columns=columns,
        rows=rows,
        row_count=event_data.get("row_count", len(rows)),
        payload=payload,
    )
    cache_result(...)
```

Pricing executors return their full state dict under an `event_data["pricing_state"]` key (alongside existing fields), so the interceptor doesn't have to know pricing internals. The `full_rows` field inside `pricing_state` is what `pricing_to_sheets` reads downstream.

### Tool registration

Register `pricing_revise` and `pricing_to_sheets` in the tool registry alongside `pricing_convert` / `pricing_export`. Both auto-flow into the `{{TOOL_INVENTORY}}` placeholder via `tool_inventory.py` (CLAUDE.md Mistakes #50). Add both to `tool_categories.py::_EXACT` under the `pricing` category.

## Knowledge Profiles

### `backend/app/services/chat/knowledge_profiles/pricing.yaml`

Add `pricing_revise` and `pricing_to_sheets` to `trigger_tools`. Extend `prompt_fragment`:

```
## Follow-up edits
For follow-up requests like "change/update/regenerate pricing":
- Call `pricing_revise` with structured `overrides`. Do NOT re-ask for the upload
  unless pricing_revise returns "No prior pricing state" (cache miss/expired).
- Pass user intent as overrides — never compute new prices yourself.
- Override mapping:
  - "increase GBP by 5%"       → overrides.percent_uplift = {"GBP": 0.05}
  - "decrease EUR by 10%"      → overrides.percent_uplift = {"EUR": -0.10}
  - "change SKU ABC-123 to 149" → overrides.sku_price_changes = [{sku: "ABC-123", usd_price: 149}]
  - "use nearest_50 for JPY"   → overrides.rounding_overrides = {"JPY": "nearest_50"}
  - "add EUR and CAD"          → overrides.currencies_to_add = ["EUR", "CAD"]
  - "drop SKU X"               → overrides.skus_to_remove = ["X"]
  - "reset / start over"       → reset = true (no other overrides needed)

## Sheets export
When the user asks to export pricing to a Google Sheet ("send to sheets",
"share as a spreadsheet", "export to Drive"), call `pricing_to_sheets`. It reads
the cached pricing state server-side and writes the full row set — you do NOT
need to pass row data. Excel + NetSuite CSV downloads from the prior run are
preserved separately. If pricing has not run yet in this conversation, run
pricing_convert / pricing_export first, THEN call pricing_to_sheets.
```

### `backend/app/services/chat/knowledge_profiles/google_sheets.yaml`

No change required — `pricing_to_sheets` is registered under the pricing profile and handles its own connector lookup. Cross-reference is unnecessary because the pricing profile triggers based on pricing tools, not on the presence of the Sheets connector.

## Frontend

**No changes required for v1.** The existing `task-output-card.tsx` renders pricing previews + download buttons regardless of whether the tool was `pricing_convert`, `pricing_export`, or `pricing_revise`. The existing `sheets-link-card.tsx` handles the Sheets URL.

**Optional polish (not v1):** small "revised" badge on the task output card when the response is a `pricing_revise` result. Adds clarity in long pricing chats.

## Testing

### Backend (pytest, `backend/.venv/bin/python -m pytest`)

`test_result_cache.py`
- `test_payload_field_round_trips` — write CachedResult with payload, read back, verify equality.
- `test_payload_field_optional_old_entries_deserialize` — feed pre-payload JSON into `from_json`, no error.
- `test_get_latest_result_by_type_filters_correctly` — cache has SuiteQL + pricing + BigQuery entries; `get_latest_result_by_type("pricing")` returns the pricing one even when SuiteQL is most recent overall.
- `test_eviction_pins_latest_per_type` — fill cache with 6 entries across 4 types (3 SuiteQL + 1 pricing + 1 BigQuery + 1 financial), add a 7th entry, verify the latest pricing/BigQuery/financial entries survive and the oldest SuiteQL is evicted.

`test_pricing_engine.py`
- `test_uplift_applied_pre_rounding_nearest_50` — JPY config with `nearest_50` rounding, base price computes to 5000, with `{JPY: 0.05}` uplift the final is 5250 (charm preserved), NOT 5197.50.
- `test_uplift_applied_pre_rounding_nearest_9` — currency with `nearest_9` rounding, base price 100 + 5% uplift = 109 (still ends in 9), NOT 105.
- `test_uplift_applied_pre_rounding_nearest_990` — currency with `nearest_990` rounding, uplift respects the X490/X990 charm pattern.
- `test_uplift_negative_value` — `{EUR: -0.10}` reduces by 10% before rounding.
- `test_uplift_multi_currency` — `{GBP: 0.05, EUR: -0.10}` in same call applies independently to each currency.
- `test_uplift_unknown_currency_no_op` — `{XYZ: 0.05}` for a currency not in config does not error and does not affect other currencies.
- `test_uplift_none_preserves_existing_behavior` — `convert_batch(items, config)` with no `uplift_by_currency` arg matches existing behavior bit-for-bit (back-compat regression test).

`test_pricing_revise.py`
- `test_cache_miss_returns_clear_error` — no prior pricing, executor returns the actionable cache-miss error string.
- `test_sku_price_change_round_trips` — initial 3 SKUs, revise one to a new USD price, engine receives the new price, output reflects it.
- `test_latest_wins_same_sku_price_change` — revise sets SKU-X to 100, second revise sets SKU-X to 120. Effective items has SKU-X at 120. `applied_overrides_log` has both entries.
- `test_percent_uplift_only_targeted_currency` — revise with `{GBP: 0.05}`, GBP rises ~5% (post-rounding), EUR unchanged.
- `test_remove_sku_drops_from_outputs` — revise `skus_to_remove=["X"]`, Excel + CSV outputs both lack X.
- `test_remove_all_skus_returns_error` — revise that removes the only remaining SKU returns the "no SKUs left" error and does not invoke the engine.
- `test_add_then_remove_same_sku_drops_it` — revise 1 adds SKU-Y, revise 2 removes SKU-Y. Effective items does not contain SKU-Y.
- `test_remove_then_add_same_sku_keeps_it` — revise 1 removes SKU-X (originally seeded), revise 2 adds SKU-X back at a new price. Effective items has SKU-X at the new price.
- `test_within_call_remove_then_add_same_sku` — single revise with both `skus_to_remove=["X"]` and `skus_to_add=[{"X", 99}]`. Effective items has SKU-X at 99 (per locked order: remove first, then add).
- `test_skus_to_add_duplicate_is_noop` — try to add an SKU already in `effective_items`, no error, audit log records `{ignored: "sku already present"}`.
- `test_currencies_to_add_unknown_currency_errors` — revise `currencies_to_add=["XYZ"]` where XYZ not in tenant config, returns the "not configured" error.
- `test_decimal_precision_through_json` — pass `usd_price: 0.1 + 0.2` (float-trap value) through tool params, verify Decimal('0.3') is what reaches the engine, not 0.30000000000000004.
- `test_cumulative_sku_changes` — revise A, then revise B. Effective items reflect both. `applied_overrides_log` has 2 entries.
- `test_replace_per_key_uplift` — revise `{GBP: 0.05}`, then revise `{GBP: 0.10}`. Effective uplift is `{GBP: 0.10}` (not 0.155).
- `test_reset_clears_overrides` — apply uplift + sku change, then revise with `reset=true`. Effective state matches seed.
- `test_reset_ignores_other_override_fields` — `reset=true` AND `overrides.percent_uplift={"GBP": 0.05}` in same call. Reset wins; uplift is dropped, `applied_overrides_log` records the reset event only.
- `test_revise_uses_fresh_tenant_config` — initial pricing_export with GBP fx_rate=1.25, then update tenant config to fx_rate=1.30 via direct DB write, then revise with empty overrides. Engine input config has GBP fx_rate=1.30 (fresh), NOT 1.25 (stale).
- `test_reset_uses_fresh_config_not_seed` — initial run with GBP at 1.25, edit GBP to 1.30, revise with `reset=true`. Effective state uses 1.30 (current), not 1.25 (seed-time).
- `test_writes_pricing_state_to_cache` — after a successful revise, the cache entry has `result_type="pricing"`, `payload.excel_file_id` matches the saved file, `payload.row_count` matches engine output.
- `test_no_response_instruction_markdown_table` — regression: pricing_revise tool result does NOT contain a markdown table in `response_instruction` (or the field is absent entirely).

`test_pricing_to_sheets.py`
- `test_no_connector_returns_actionable_error_FIRST` — Sheets connector missing/inactive AND no pricing run. Executor returns the connector-setup error, NOT the cache-miss error (regression for the connector-check-first ordering decision).
- `test_cache_miss_returns_error_when_connector_present` — connector OK but no pricing run, executor returns the cache-miss error.
- `test_writes_full_row_set_via_excel_reparse` — initial run with 50 SKUs, call pricing_to_sheets, executor calls `TaskFileService.get_file(payload.excel_file_id)`, parses with openpyxl, writes 50 data rows + 1 header row to Sheets.
- `test_uses_excel_file_not_cache_for_rows` — `payload` has `excel_file_id` and `row_count` but NO `full_rows` field. Executor still produces correct Sheets output.
- `test_excel_file_missing_returns_error` — payload references an excel_file_id that no longer exists (deleted/expired). Executor returns a clear error.
- `test_does_not_bump_cache` — pricing_to_sheets call does not write a new cache entry (read-only consumer).
- `test_emits_sheets_link_event` — SSE stream contains a `sheets_link` event with the spreadsheet URL.

`test_orchestrator_paths.py`
- `test_pricing_intercept_writes_payload` — full orchestrator turn for pricing_convert/export, cached entry has `result_type="pricing"`, non-empty `payload.excel_file_id` and `payload.row_count`.
- `test_pricing_to_sheets_does_not_write_cache` — pricing_to_sheets call does not bump cache (read-only consumer).
- `test_conversation_id_in_executor_context` — executor receives `context["conversation_id"]` (regression for the plumbing change).

`test_pricing_executors_cleanup.py` (new file for the response_instruction cleanup)
- `test_pricing_convert_no_markdown_table_in_result` — regression: pricing_convert_execute return value does NOT include `response_instruction` containing a markdown table. (Field may still exist for non-table guidance, but no `|---|` row separators.)
- `test_pricing_export_no_markdown_table_in_result` — same for pricing_export_execute.
- `test_pricing_convert_preview_field_unchanged` — the `preview` field shape (list of dicts with SKU/USD/currency keys) is preserved so the frontend card still renders.

### Vs-MCP benchmark

Add 3 cases to `backend/tests/agent_benchmarks/benchmark_cases/vs_mcp/`:
- `pricing_followup_percent_uplift` — initial run + "increase GBP by 5% and regenerate" + assert agent calls `pricing_revise` with `overrides.percent_uplift={"GBP": 0.05}` and the GBP delta in the resulting cached payload is correct.
- `pricing_to_sheets_export` — initial run + "export to a Google Sheet" + assert agent calls `pricing_to_sheets` (NOT manual sheets_create+sheets_write_range composition).
- `pricing_revise_after_unrelated_turn` — initial pricing run, then a SuiteQL turn, then "increase GBP by 5%". Confirms `get_latest_result_by_type` returns the pricing entry even though SuiteQL is more recent.

Required to pass per CLAUDE.md Mistakes #31 (CI gate).

### Frontend (vitest, `cd frontend && npx vitest run`)

No new tests strictly required for v1 (no UI changes). If the optional "revised" badge ships, add `task-output-card.test.tsx::renders revised badge`.

## Acceptance Criteria

From the ClickUp ticket, mapped to this design:

1. ✅ Initial pricing calculation continues to work for uploaded Excel and inline item input — untouched.
2. ✅ Follow-up prompts can update prior pricing results without re-uploading — `pricing_revise` reads cached payload.
3. ✅ Follow-up edits support: SKU/base price changes, currency-specific FX/VAT/rounding changes, percent uplifts/discounts, adding/removing SKUs — all in the override schema.
4. ✅ Updated output uses the deterministic backend pricing engine where applicable; LLM orchestrates intent and parameters, not final prices.
5. ✅ Updated result renders in chat with a preview table — same `task_output` event shape as initial run.
6. ✅ Updated Excel download — written via existing `_file_svc.save_output`.
7. ✅ Updated NetSuite CSV download — same path.
8. ✅ Export the updated result to Google Sheets — agent composes `sheets_create` + `sheets_write_range`.
9. ✅ Cache miss / expired state — `pricing_revise` returns clear error, profile prompt instructs agent to ask for the upload/items.
10. ✅ Regression tests — covered by `test_pricing_revise.py` + 2 new vs-MCP benchmark cases.

## Distribution Plan

Existing deployment pipeline covers this — no new artifacts. Backend image build + GCP staging deploy via the standard saas-deployment skill flow. After staging passes the vs-MCP benchmark, merge to main → frontend redeploy not required (no FE changes for v1).

## Dependencies

- No new packages.
- Tenant must have a Google Sheets connector configured (`mcp_connectors.provider = "google_sheets"`) to use the Sheets export path. Without it, `sheets_create` returns a clear error and the agent falls back to Excel + CSV downloads only.
- Pricing config must already define every currency the user mentions in `currencies_to_add` overrides — the override does not seed new currency configs.

## Open Questions

1. **Audit log lineage.** `PricingConversionLog` has no revision-lineage column. v1 stores `applied_overrides_log` inside `config_snapshot` JSON to avoid a migration. If product wants queryable revision history, add a `revised_from` FK + `applied_overrides` JSONB column in v2.
2. **Cache TTL / scenario compare.** 30-min TTL is fine for one revise chain. Compare-back-to-an-earlier-revision requires explicit save/restore — out of scope.
3. **Sheet update vs. create.** v1 always creates a new sheet. If users want to update an existing sheet ("update the same sheet I made earlier"), we'd need to cache the spreadsheet_id alongside the pricing payload and add an `update: true` mode to `pricing_to_sheets`. Punt unless requested.

## Next Steps

Implementation order matches dependency order:

1. Schema change to `CachedResult` (+ `payload`) + `get_latest_result_by_type` helper + new eviction policy (`MAX_RESULTS_PER_CONVERSATION` 3→6, per-type pinning). Tests: round-trip, old-entry compatibility, type-filter, pin-per-type eviction.
2. Engine extension: `convert_batch` / `convert_single` accept `uplift_by_currency`. Tests: uplift pre-rounding for `nearest_50` / `nearest_9` / `nearest_990`, negative uplift, multi-currency, unknown-currency no-op, None-preserves-existing-behavior.
3. Add `conversation_id` to the executor context dict in the orchestrator's tool dispatch path. Single-line plumbing change; regression test in `test_orchestrator_paths.py::test_conversation_id_in_executor_context`.
4. Pricing executor changes — `pricing_convert` and `pricing_export` emit `pricing_state` (with `seed_*` / `effective_*` / `excel_file_id` / `row_count`) in event_data. Drop the `response_instruction` markdown-table builder (per eng review D2). Tests: `test_pricing_executors_cleanup.py`.
5. Orchestrator interceptor populates `payload` and sets `result_type="pricing"` for `_PRICING_WRITE_TOOLS`.
6. New `pricing_revise` executor + tool registration + categorization. `PricingState` Pydantic model. Full test suite per Testing section.
7. New `pricing_to_sheets` executor — connector-check-first, Excel re-parse via `TaskFileService.get_file` + `openpyxl`. Full test suite per Testing section.
8. `pricing.yaml` profile fragment update (+ trigger_tools `pricing_revise` and `pricing_to_sheets`). Drop the "show this exact table" instructions if any survive the executor cleanup.
9. Vs-MCP benchmark cases (3 new) — assert agent calls `pricing_revise` / `pricing_to_sheets` (not manual sheets composition) and that `get_latest_result_by_type` survives a SuiteQL turn between two pricing turns.
10. Staging deploy + benchmark gate + merge.

## What Already Exists (reuse map)

This spec deliberately reuses existing primitives rather than building parallel infrastructure:

| Sub-problem | Existing code reused | New work |
|-------------|---------------------|----------|
| Cache infra (Redis, TTL, eviction, message-id keying) | `result_cache.py` | One field (`payload`) + one helper (`get_latest_result_by_type`) + eviction policy tweak |
| Pricing math (FX, VAT, charm rounding) | `pricing_engine.py::convert_batch/single` | Optional `uplift_by_currency` kwarg, applied pre-rounding |
| Excel generation (default 3-sheet workbook) | `template_filler.py::generate_default_output` | None — reused as-is |
| NetSuite CSV generation | `template_filler.py::generate_netsuite_csv` | None — reused as-is |
| File save/retrieve | `TaskFileService::save_output / get_file` | None — reused as-is |
| Tool result interception → SSE → frontend card | `orchestrator.py::_intercept_tool_result` + `_on_tool_intercepted` | One conditional branch for `_PRICING_WRITE_TOOLS` to set `result_type="pricing"` and pipe `payload` |
| Sheets create + write + share + drive scoping | `sheets_tools.py::sheets_create_execute`, `sheets_service.py::write_range/share_spreadsheet` | None at the service layer — `pricing_to_sheets` calls these directly |
| Frontend pricing card | `task-output-card.tsx` | None — preview shape unchanged |
| Frontend Sheets link card | `sheets-link-card.tsx` | None — `sheets_link` SSE event already supported |
| Audit log table | `PricingConversionLog` | None — `applied_overrides_log` lives in existing `config_snapshot` JSON |
| Knowledge profiles + tool inventory + categorization | `knowledge_profiles/`, `tool_inventory.py`, `tool_categories.py` | One profile fragment update + two new tool-category entries |

**No parallel rebuild.** The spec adds: 2 new tools, 1 new helper, 1 cache field, 1 engine kwarg, 1 cleanup of existing tools. The rest is plumbing.

## Failure Modes (per new codepath)

| Codepath | Realistic failure | Test? | Error handling? | User-visible? |
|----------|------------------|-------|-----------------|---------------|
| `get_latest_result_by_type` returns None | Cache TTL expired mid-conversation | ✅ test_cache_miss_returns_clear_error | ✅ Clear "no prior pricing" error | ✅ Yes — agent quotes the error |
| Eviction drops pricing under heavy mixed-tool use | 6+ non-pricing turns + active pricing | ✅ test_eviction_pins_latest_per_type | ✅ Pin protects pricing | ❌ Silent (works as designed) |
| Engine called with `uplift_by_currency` for unknown currency | LLM hallucinates a currency | ✅ test_uplift_unknown_currency_no_op | ✅ No-op, no error | ❌ Silent — appears in logs |
| `pricing_revise` invoked with empty effective_items after removal | User removes the last SKU | ✅ test_remove_all_skus_returns_error | ✅ Clear error | ✅ Yes |
| `currencies_to_add` references un-configured currency | LLM extrapolates from Settings UI | ✅ test_currencies_to_add_unknown_currency_errors | ✅ Clear error directing to Settings | ✅ Yes |
| Float precision drift through JSON | LLM emits `0.1+0.2` style values | ✅ test_decimal_precision_through_json | ✅ Decimal(str(v)) conversion | ❌ Silent if not caught — pricing wrong by sub-cent |
| `pricing_to_sheets` connector missing | Tenant hasn't configured Sheets | ✅ test_no_connector_returns_actionable_error_FIRST | ✅ Clear error directing to Settings | ✅ Yes |
| `pricing_to_sheets` Excel file deleted from File Cabinet | Manual cleanup or expiry | ✅ test_excel_file_missing_returns_error | ✅ Clear error | ✅ Yes |
| Tenant config edited mid-conversation | User changes FX rates in Settings | ✅ test_revise_uses_fresh_tenant_config | ✅ Fresh fetch on every revise | ❌ Silent (works as designed — fresh config IS the user's intent) |
| Sheets API rate limit / 429 | Heavy concurrent Sheets exports | ❌ Not covered | ❌ Generic exception → 500 | ✅ Yes (generic error) |
| Excel parse failure (corrupted file) | File corruption mid-flight | ❌ Not covered | ❌ Generic exception → 500 | ✅ Yes (generic error) |
| Engine stack overflow on huge catalog (>50K SKUs) | Tenant uploads enormous catalog | ❌ Not covered | ❌ Likely OOM in worker | ✅ Yes (timeout) |

**Critical gaps:** none. The three uncovered failure modes are all generic-exception paths that fall back to existing 500 handling. Acceptable for v1.

## NOT in Scope (deferred)

| Item | Why deferred |
|------|--------------|
| Scenario-compare across revisions | Cache TTL 30min × 6 entries supports it briefly; full scenario-compare needs explicit save/restore. Defer until requested. |
| Update existing spreadsheet vs. always create | Needs spreadsheet_id storage in payload + `update: true` mode. Punt unless requested. |
| Per-row override syntax (conditional uplifts on product groups) | Engine doesn't support per-row uplift today; would need engine changes. Defer. |
| Removing markdown-table pattern from non-pricing tools | This cleanup is pricing-specific. Other tools (BigQuery, SuiteQL) do not have the same pattern in the same way; separate audit. |
| `revised_from` FK column on `PricingConversionLog` | Lineage lives in JSON `applied_overrides_log` for v1. Add column in v2 if queryable revision history is needed. |
| Sheets API rate-limit handling beyond generic 5xx | Single-tenant, non-bulk usage — unlikely to hit limits in v1. Add structured retry if it becomes an issue. |
| Excel parse error handling beyond generic 5xx | Excel corruption is rare; generic error path is fine for v1. |
| Frontend "revised" badge on task-output-card | Optional polish; doesn't block the feature. |

## Worktree Parallelization

Most steps depend on the previous one. Limited parallelization opportunity.

| Step | Modules touched | Depends on |
|------|----------------|------------|
| 1. Cache schema + helper + eviction | `services/chat/result_cache.py` | — |
| 2. Engine extension (uplift kwarg) | `services/pricing_engine.py` | — |
| 3. conversation_id plumbing in executor context | `services/chat/orchestrator.py` (dispatch path only) | — |
| 4. Pricing executor changes (pricing_state emit + cleanup) | `mcp/tools/pricing_tools.py` | 2 (engine kwarg) |
| 5. Orchestrator interceptor for pricing payload | `services/chat/orchestrator.py` (intercept path) | 1 (cache field), 4 (pricing_state shape) |
| 6. pricing_revise executor + tool registration | `mcp/tools/pricing_tools.py`, `tool_registry.py`, `tool_categories.py` | 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 |
| 7. pricing_to_sheets executor + tool registration | `mcp/tools/pricing_tools.py`, `tool_registry.py`, `tool_categories.py` | 1, 3, 4 |
| 8. pricing.yaml profile fragment | `services/chat/knowledge_profiles/pricing.yaml` | 6, 7 (tools must exist before trigger_tools list works) |
| 9. Vs-MCP benchmark cases | `tests/agent_benchmarks/benchmark_cases/vs_mcp/` | 6, 7, 8 |

**Parallel lanes:**
- **Lane A (cache):** step 1 — independent
- **Lane B (engine):** step 2 — independent
- **Lane C (orchestrator plumbing):** step 3 — independent of A and B
- All three (A + B + C) can run in parallel worktrees.
- **Lane D (executors + tools):** steps 4-7 — must wait for A, B, C to merge. Within D, step 6 and 7 share `pricing_tools.py` so they should run sequentially (or with careful coordination).
- **Lane E (profile + benchmark):** steps 8-9 — sequential, depends on D.

**Conflict flags:**
- Steps 6 and 7 both touch `pricing_tools.py` and the tool registry — run sequentially OR coordinate via shared scaffolding commit first.
- Steps 3 and 5 both touch `orchestrator.py` (different sections) — Lane C lands first, Lane D rebases.

**Recommended execution:** A + B + C in parallel worktrees, merge all three to main, then sequential D, then E. Realistic CC time: ~30 min for parallel lanes, ~60 min sequential after merge.

---

## Reviewer Concerns (resolved)

**Pass 1 — adversarial reviewer (during /office-hours):**

1. ✅ `get_latest_result` could return non-pricing results → resolved by `get_latest_result_by_type`.
2. ✅ Sheets export needed full row set, not preview → resolved by server-side `pricing_to_sheets` (re-parse Excel per eng review D1).
3. ✅ Post-multiply uplift broke charm-price rounding → resolved by engine-level pre-rounding `uplift_by_currency`.
4. ✅ Cache eviction could drop pricing under cross-tool sessions → resolved by per-type pinning + bumped MAX (3→6).
5. ✅ Cumulative override semantics ambiguous → locked: SKU-set ops accumulate, FX/VAT/rounding/uplift replace per key, explicit `reset` flag.
6. ✅ `reference_message_id` semantics tangled with "pending" cache key → resolved by dropping the param from v1.
7. ✅ `currencies_to_add` for un-configured currency → explicit error contract.
8. ✅ Decimal precision through JSON → executor converts via `Decimal(str(v))`, tool description documents it.
9. ✅ `response_instruction` markdown-table inheritance → `pricing_revise` explicitly omits the pattern.

**Pass 2 — eng review (this pass):**

10. ✅ (D1) Storing full row set in Redis would balloon Upstash costs on large catalogs → resolved by re-parsing the saved Excel file in `pricing_to_sheets`. Cache only holds `excel_file_id` + headers + row count.
11. ✅ (D2) Markdown-table cleanup was punted on `pricing_convert/export` → folded into v1 scope (delete `response_instruction` from both, add regression tests).
12. ✅ (D3) Per-SKU conflict resolution was vague → locked: latest-wins on `sku_price_changes`; `skus_to_add` for present SKU = silent no-op; `skus_to_remove` then add re-adds; within-call order is remove → add → price-change.
13. ✅ (D4) Cached `seed_config` would create stale-FX foot-gun when users edit Settings mid-conversation → fetch fresh tenant config on every revise; remove `seed_config` from cache. Audit lineage stays in `PricingConversionLog`.
14. ✅ (Plumbing) `conversation_id` not threaded into executor context → spec'd as a single-line addition to the orchestrator dispatch path with a regression test.
15. ✅ (Connector ordering) `pricing_to_sheets` should check connector before cache so users get the right setup-vs-session error → ordering locked in executor responsibilities.
16. ✅ (Empty items) `effective_items` empty after removals could crash the engine → explicit error contract before engine call.
17. ✅ (Code quality) `_PRICING_WRITE_TOOLS` set documented; `PricingState` renamed for accuracy; cache-miss error extracted to module constant.

---

## GSTACK REVIEW REPORT

| Review | Trigger | Why | Runs | Status | Findings |
|--------|---------|-----|------|--------|----------|
| CEO Review | `/plan-ceo-review` | Scope & strategy | 0 | — | not run (scope inherited from ClickUp ticket) |
| Eng Review | `/plan-eng-review` | Architecture & tests (required) | 1 | CLEAR (PLAN) | 7 issues, 0 critical gaps, 4 user decisions resolved (D1-D4) |
| Design Review | `/plan-design-review` | UI/UX gaps | 0 | — | n/a (no FE changes) |
| DX Review | `/plan-devex-review` | Developer experience gaps | 0 | — | n/a (internal tool) |
| Codex Review | `/codex review` | Independent 2nd opinion | 0 | — | not run (skipped per fast-path) |

- **CROSS-MODEL:** /office-hours adversarial reviewer (Claude subagent) caught 9 issues; /plan-eng-review caught 7 more. All 16 resolved in spec. No outside-voice (Codex) run.
- **UNRESOLVED:** 0
- **VERDICT:** ENG CLEARED — ready to implement. Sequential execution per "Worktree Parallelization" section.
